content.full_text:
/

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 16, 1986

Joseph R. Symkowick
Chief Counsel
CA State Department of Education
P.O. Box 944272
Sacramento, CA 94244-2720
Re: FPPC No. A-86-114

Dear Mr. Symkowick:

This is in reply to your letter dated April 7, 1986, in which you asked for advice on behalf of Superintendent of Public Instruction Bill Honig.

You asked if Mr. Honig would incur any disclosure obligations under the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act in connection with use of his state assigned car to travel to campaign events. You indicated that because Mr. Honig is up for re-election this year, he will attend campaign events at various times. As a statewide constitutional officer identified in the succession he uses a state assigned vehicle and is accompanied by a state police officer in going from campaign event to business and vice versa; that Mr. Honig is always carrying out his duties as Superintendent of Public Instruction and is in constant contact with management at the Department of Education.

We have determined that, because use of the state vehicle and its communications equipment is necessary at all times to Mr. Honig's protection and performance of his official duties, Mr. Honig has not received a campaign contribution from the state and, therefore, has no obligations in connection with such use.

Thank you for your inquiry. Please call me if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

Jeanne Pritchard
Division Chief, Technical
Assistance & Analysis Division

JP:kt

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

CALIFORNIA STATE DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION                    Bill Honig

721 Capitol Mall; P.O. Box 944272                                    Superintendent

Sacramento, CA 94244-2720                                            of Public Instruction

April 7, 1986


Jean Pritchard, Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

RE: USE OF STATE-ASSIGNED VEHICLE

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

I am writing this letter as a followup to our phone
conversation of April 2, 1986.

As you know, I am Counsel to the Department of Education
and Bill Honig, Superintendent of Public Instruction. Mr.
Honig is a statewide elected constitutional officer serving
currently in the fourth year of his first four-year term.
He is running for re-election at this time. As I explained
to you as a constitutional officer identified in the
succession, Mr. Honig is assigned a state unmarked vehicle
that is usually driven by an assigned state policeman who
accompanies him for protection. Mr. Honig is always
carrying out his duties as Superintendent of Public
Instruction and is in constant contact with management at
the Department of Education. Mr. Honig is also campaigning
and at various times throughout the day will attend
campaign events. In going from campaign event to business
and vice versa, he uses the state-assigned vehicle and is
accompanied by the state police officer. Often times while
at a campaign event, he will be contacted by telephone for
state related business purposes.

On behalf of Mr. Honig I have contacted the Department
of Personnel Administration and based on that contact it
is my understanding that nothing further is required of
Mr. Honig than to account for the campaign related use of
the state vehicle. This accounting would be reported to
the Internal Revenue Service in accordance with their rules
and regulations (see attached memorandum to Joyce Whiten,
Department of Personnel Administration). My telephonic
inquiry to you was for the purpose of ascertaining whether
any further reporting or other requirements would be placed
upon Mr. Honig's use of the state vehicle in relation to
the campaign. You indicated that there had been no
previous advice with regard to this matter and asked me to
prepare this letter to you.

JEAN PRITCHARD
April 7, 1986
Page 2


Due to the oversight functions of the Fair Political
Practices Commission regarding campaign activities, we
would request your advice regarding what, if anything, is
required of Mr. Honig in relation to his use of the state-
assigned vehicle for campaign purposes.  Should you have
any need for further information, please do not hesitate to
contact me.  Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Sincerely,

JOSEPH R. SYMKOWICK
Chief Counsel
Legal Office
445-4694

JRS:dh

/                                                                                9
State of California                                                Department of Education

Memorandum

To    :    JOYCE WHITEN                                        Date  :  March 20, 1986
         Department of Personnel
         Administration                                        File No.:


From  :    Legal Office, Room 552 (5-4694)


Subject:   Reporting of Personal Car Use to Internal Revenue
           Service


         The purpose of this memorandum is to confirm my previous
         phone conversation with you.

         Superintendent of Public Instruction, Bill Honig is
         assigned a state car on a permanent basis. He will be
         required to attend various functions as a result of his re-
         election campaign. He anticipates that in certain
         instances he will be using the state-assigned vehicle to go
         to and from these functions. It is my understanding that
         you concur that such a use of the state vehicle is outside
         the realm of his responsibility as State Superintendent of
         Public Instruction and should accordingly be reported to
         the Internal Revenue Service.

         By this memorandum we are confirming our understanding.
         The superintendent will report any campaign related use of
         the vehicle to the Internal Revenue Service in accordance
         with applicable rules and regulations. We would appreciate
         it if you would inform us if you determine that any other
         reporting is necessary.


         [illegible signature]
         JOSEPH R. SYMROWICK
         Chief Counsel

         JRS:dh
